description: A merge cell fed from a FORMULA (not a plain cell) still receives one merge per settled cone. This is the additive async-scheduler fixture (design §9.1): the other merge_*  fixtures feed from a plain cell, and cells are the synchronous input layer, so as written they never exercise the async-specific path -- the fold is synchronous and the feed effect's only asynchrony is when it reruns. Here the feed reads a computed formula d = a, so on an async model the feed effect cannot run until that formula RESOLVES, which is exactly the path the async scheduler's coalescing claim covers: N invalidations in one batch must collapse to ONE effect rerun even when the read awaits. The discriminator is the same as merge_per_settled_cone -- merges_of under the non-idempotent Sum policy -- because a binding that reran the feed per write, or per formula recompute, folds too many ops and lands on the wrong value. The formula acquires the edge to a; the merge cell stays edge-free. Contract: #lzmergefeed.

expect.note: baseline: the effect runs once on creation, reads the formula d = a = 0, and folds it in. The edge belongs to feed (via d); acc stays edge-free.

THE discriminating step. Three writes invalidate d once (batch coalesces the cascade), the feed reruns once, and ONE merge folds the settled value. A binding that reran the feed per write -- or per async formula recompute -- shows more than 2, even reading through an awaited formula.
the single merge folded the SETTLED formula value d = 3, not the sequence 1,2,3
outside a batch every write is its own settled cone, so the formula resolves to 10 and the feed folds once more
0 + 3 + 10 under Sum, and acc never acquired a dependency edge despite being fed through a formula
